rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

100 million dollar renovation falls short. Let's start with the positive.  Staff. They tried to make things right. And they did quickly! Now... the lobby area and pool were beautiful.  The rooms were not great. Air did not stay cool in evening.  We had to request a long mirror. The storage and wardrobe is not enough for a longer stay. The electric kept going on and off. Lighting in rooms is terrible and there are no automatic shades or all.off electric buttons or easy on off lights.  Everything is manual. They do not have service elevators so you are in the thick of it with carts. The robes are thin and scratchy.  The property next door was under construction so noise is an issue at times. It certainly is not higher end. The little things like slippers turn down service cozy robes welcome plate or easy on off lights from your bed just were not there. I do not recommend this location for events. They ran out of food.  Maybe over time they can get it to the scale they are trying to convey.
First day my room was behind the lift and I had a headache but staff did not refund me that stay . I am disappointed
Felt like it needs professional hotel managers

Booked several rooms for my family in early January. Location is great and the ground floor was refreshingly different to other hotels.  But the whole place felt like it was run by amateurs.  When you are paying around one thousand per night, you want the place to be run by professionals.    We booked a balcony suite.  Several flaws:  1)  If you decide to book one, ask for an even numbered one.  My wife and I were on a high floor in an odd numbered one (I forget the number) and the terrace was continously smelling of food from the restaurant's extraction directly below.  What is the point of booking a suite with a terrace and then not being able to use the terrace!  2) The room was spacious but if you plan on reading in the living room, I suggest you bring along a lamp.  There are two bleak wall lights and that's it.  So forget reading unless you bring your own light.     Breakfast menu is limited and more surprisingly, rigid.  I asked for plain yogurt and they said they only had it with granola....... I asked for a muffin and they said they only had croissants...  Not what one expects of a luxury hotel.     What really made me doubt about returning was the maid service.  We had no problem with the cleaning..... when the room was cleaned.  The problem is that over a five night stay, my son's room was not cleaned on two occasions and the other times it was cleaned at 5 pm!  When I mentioned this at check out, the answer was "thank you for letting us know".  Mind blowing.  How about "I'm sorry"?  How about "Let me have a gesture with your bill"......  Curiously enough, the lady in front had the same complaint so not getting properly timed cleaning service seems to be a thing in this hotel.     Would I go back?  Not so sure.

Good afternoon,  Thank you for taking the time to prove your feedback. We do appreciate it and take your comments seriously. I apologize that we fell short of your expectations. I would love the opportunity to speak with you personally if you are open to that. Thank you. Crispy- Director Guest Experience

Good: Following the good reviews I was really looking forward to staying here. The hotel itself is very aesthetic. However, the staff were unfriendly, extremely slow and even gave us the wrong bags to leave with on checking out seriously questioning the security of this hotel. The bar was not stocked with simple drinks for the quality of this hotel. It is also right next to an extremely loud building site so say goodbye to any relaxing sunbathing. They did not top up the cotton pads over the course of our stay suggesting lack of attention to detail. This is over priced massively for the experience we had. So disappointed    Bad: Everything apart from the aesthetic
Never again - does the Proper brand a disservice

Good: Great location and proximity to beach.    Bad: Only one elevator worked. Electricity went out for several hours and staff did nothing to help guests. The rooms are tired. Definitely not what I expect from the Proper brand.

Don't park a car at this hotel. It took 45 minutes - total confusion with a stupid app, unhelpful desk staff. The person in front of me waiting for a car had to take her husband to the hospital with an Uber. I finally asked to speak to a manager. Won't stay again. Too many other great hotels in South Beach with great service
The holding fee is ridiculously high
Over styled over priced uncomfortable that lacks the basics

VERY DISAPPOINTING   Just finishing up a 3 night stay and we won’t return.  This is a typical chain hotel exuding style over substance. It’s like IKEA heaven throughout with modern design furnishings that look great in a magazine or on a glitzy insta post but lack comfort and luxury to be enjoyed at leisure.   We paid premium for an oceanfront suite with balcony. Don’t bother as the ocean is way beyond the front of the room with pool, an ugly accommodation block a garden and a public walkway between you and the beach!   Additionally the room was billed as a corner suite with the idea of views and two balconies. Both faced the service entrance with constant noise from staff and maintenance who shouted and laughed at all hours of day and night.  There is construction all around the hotel too.  The room again was big on style low on comfort. The TV remotes worked intermittently due to placing of receiver in a hidden position. The dimmer light switches were tricky to operate. There was a coffee machine with no cups. The tiny fridge only had beverage for sale no room to store water. The shower was good but the shower curtain one of those cheap plastic types like you get at Walmart!  There’s no dressing table or any mirror to sit at to do hair or makeup. There’s one tiny and I mean tiny trash can in the bathroom.   One of the other main gripes was the intermittent failure of the elevators. When one worked the other didn’t so often long queues and wait times all through our stay.  There seems to be no service lift so the small lifts were often shared with laundry/cleaning/room service.    Apart from the comfortable bed and great shower there’s nothing that makes this a luxurious suite and was definitely not worth the premium price we paid.    The staff were pleasant though and the beach team in particular were great (especially Andrew) and when we complained about Tv remotes or lack of coffee cups the guest services team and their colleagues were very polite and prompt to deal with.    This hotel isn’t 5 star and is over styled at the expense of basics and comfort.

Thank you Paul,  for taking the time to share such detailed feedback about your recent three-night stay with us. Your comments are extremely valuable, and it is clear that we fell short of delivering the comfortable, relaxing experience you were expecting.  It is important to us as we are always striving to elevate our services levels.   Please accept our sincere apologies for the issues you encountered, particularly with the room location and views, elevator reliability, noise, and the in-room amenities and layout. This is not the impression we want to give of our property, and your description highlights several areas where we need to improve both our facilities and our maintenance checks.    We are especially sorry that the suite did not match your expectations of a premium, oceanfront experience and that the noise from service areas and surrounding construction disturbed your stay. Your feedback on the room design, storage, mirrors, trash cans, and minibar setup has been shared with our management and housekeeping teams so we can review how these spaces are equipped and address the comfort issues you raised.  On a positive note, we are glad to read your kind words about our staff, particularly our beach team and Andrew, and that they were able to assist you promptly and courteously when concerns were raised. Recognizing and reinforcing this level of service is important to us, and your feedback will be shared with them.  If you are open to it, we would truly appreciate the opportunity to speak with you directly so we can learn more and see what we can do to restore your confidence in us. Please feel free to contact me.    Crispy Soloperto, Director Guest Experience

Good: Bel hôtel de Miami Beach avec tout ce que l’on peut souhaiter piscine et plage privée mais ça ne fait pas tout    Bad: Pas de douche possible dans l’hôtel une fois la chambre rendue alors que l’hôtel est au bord de mer, même à la piscine il n’y a pas de douche, même pas un malheureux jet d’eau pour se rincer. On nous a proposé d’aller nous laver à la douche municipale du front de mer !!!! Après avoir payé 800 euros par jour on nous propose d’aller nous laver à la douche de la ville sans scrupule. Nous pouvions prendre un late check out qui n’allait que jusque 17h alors que notre avion était à 23h. Même en payant 175 euros de plus il nous fallait nous changer dans les toilettes du restaurant avant de partir ! Jamais vu ça ! Chambre petit avec peu de rangement Salle de bain très petite sans fenêtre et pas d’espace pour poser les affaires

Thoroughly mediocre. The common areas smelled like grease, the elevators were painfully slow (no service elevators, so staff going up and down constantly), and there were little things off pretty consistently (ie there was hand soap in the shower instead of shower gel).    Staff was kind, the pool / beach areas were well done, and the few bites we had were solid. Definitely overpriced for what the hotel quality, and not in line with other Proper properties I’ve stayed in.

So we booked from 27th to 31 DEC 2025, and knew ahead of time the cost was going to be high during this time.  We booked the cheapest room, city view 2 queens.  Arrival and check in was quick, the person checking us in was quick and efficient but that's about it.  There are one set of elevators for the main tower.  We were on fifth floor facing the roof of the hotel out the side.  As someone said in a review, the room, while large, lacked any real furniture but the two beds which were very comfortable as the mattress is newish.  (hotel really just opened up last winter 2025).  Opposite the beds are simple metal storage unit like something at Ikea.  There is a tiny table with two basic chairs.  No furniture for the large space to lounge or sit.  Other than a few shelves on the metal bookcase, there are no hooks and especially bad no hooks in the small bathroom at all.  You have to put hand towels or towels over shower door.  The vanity is super small with no counter space.  Very odd.  There are built ins for hanging clothes though.  We also experience very spotty housekeeping.  One day they didn't come and had to call, she did quick work and left incomplete set of towels and didn't empty trash.  Next day they came at 6pm when we were getting ready for dinner.  Odd. I did mention this to desk.  The bar area was ok, and semi busy at night for drinks.  In morning it was just used for coffee and tea.  We ate breakfast once in cafe by pool and luckily got in before huge lines formed.  The breakfast was unremarkable and expensive.  We ended up going to the Pura Vida down the street to order great breakfast on the app and bring it back to eat in the bar area.  Best tip right there.  The pool area was the best part -- great staff.  You should get there 8AM to get some chairs and they will set it up -- please tip them.  Great service although you have to really call their attention to come take an order.  Beach was nice although they will set up chairs and umbrella in this super crowded area with others and facing away from beach.  Usually we prefer the beach but from where you sat you couldn't see it and faced the sun.  The pool was the spot to be.  We never had anyone really greet or care to ask how stay was going.  The staff at times seemed to still be trying to coordinate their service with each other.  Our last day we smelled raw sewage coming from the side of the property on sidewalk and had to sidestep it -- what was that about.  In final, it has nice pool spot with calm music and not a crazy scene like some larger hotels have with loud music so that alone is work but the value is not here for this place, sadly.
Bon potentiel mais peut mieux faire

Good: L'emplacement : l'Hôtel est très bien situé. La piscine est exceptionnelle et la musique n'y est pas trop fort contrairement aux hôtels voisins. Le confort des chambres. Il faut être du côté droit de l'hôtel la vue est dégagée (certes sur un chantier) mais à gauche la vue donne sur l'hôtel à coté.    Bad: Personnel aimable mais inefficace et surtout peu arrangeant. 5 jours passés avec 3 chambres louées et il a fallu se changer le jour du départ dans les toilettes. Certains transats à la piscine sont destinés à une location supplémentaire et ne sont pas accessibles. L'éclairage des chambres reste toujours un mystère. Les petits déjeuners : sur le trottoir devant l'hôtel moitié prix et bien meilleurs.

I was out of my room from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m., yet the room was not cleaned. At 5:00 p.m., before leaving for dinner, I went downstairs to request housekeeping. When I returned at 9:00 p.m., the room was cleaned half way—sand on the floor and dirty dishes still present.    I went back downstairs simply to request water, which arrived around 9:30 p.m. Then passed 11:00 p.m., a housekeeper knocked on my door asking to clean the room, waking up my child in the process. I understand that hotels can get busy, but offering to clean a room at 11:00 at night is completely unacceptable. If management truly wanted to make things right, someone should have been sent earlier in the evening. Instead, the situation was handled poorly and made worse.    We also had a bad experience at the restaurant. We came for breakfast, and despite the restaurant being about 75% empty, we waited 20 minutes to be seated. I eventually spoke to a gentleman named Michael, who said he was a manager. He told us they were working to get everyone seated and then walked away. We left shortly after, as there are many nearby options with far better service.    Another thing my wife noticed was the overall lack of hospitality. Staff never greeted guests—no “good morning,” “good afternoon,” or even a smile when passing by. The only exception was the bell staff and valet team, who were genuinely friendly and helpful. I feel bad leaving a one-star review because of them.   I did tip absolutely everyone that did something positive including leaving money in the room before every cleaning.       That said, this is not, by any standard, a high-end hotel experience Ritz a block away was cheaper for the same nights.  I used my Marriott points to stay at this hotel  since they accept them.   Don’t expect any Marriott perks like free breakfast or bonus points.  They don’t honor them at this place (Titanium status).   I travel frequently and stay over 80 nights a year in hotels on average. Management here clearly needs serious training. While I love the location, there are too many better options in the same proximity , and it will be an easy decision not to stay here again.

L’hotel è carino ed è situato in un’ottima posizione centrale.  La nostra camera tuttavia era abbastanza piccola e angusta con vista su un condominio in costruzione affianco.  La zona piscina andrebbe un pochino migliorata, la pulizia è abbastanza scarsa ( ma diciamo che è un problema comunque negli States)  Tutto sommato siamo rimasti soddisfatti ma abbiamo pagato 400 dollari a notte, non vale assolutamente un centesimo in più  Siamo rimasti anche scandalizzati dal fatto che ci abbiano messo in conto 48 $ per due sacchetti di patatine al mini bar in camera

Great Location and Food but Lacking in Service

We stayed at the Shelborne for a quick weekend getaway in Miami. The location of the hotel is very central in Miami Beach and the rooms/facilities are well-designed. The food was excellent across the board and there are a multitude of healthy and delicious dining options. The main area the hotel was lacking was in service. The staff are all friendly but didn’t seem in sync or organized. There were multiple items ordered whether via room service or at the pool/beach that never showed up or took repeated reminders over 45 min+. For such a well-staffed establishment, it was surprisingly difficult to get the attention of the staff and we ended up just serving ourselves whenever possible. We’ll probably give them another shot because it is a relatively new spot and they seem to be working out a lot of the issues.

Please don’t hesitate to reach out directly for any future reservations so we can ensure your visit is flawless.    Sincerely,  —Front Office Team at The Shelborne by Proper

Hôtel magnifique mais qui n’est malheureusement pas terminé.  Il y a un défilé constant d’ouvriers à la piscine  et les bruits des travaux sont bruyants et très désagréables. Nous y avons séjourné 3 jours et chaque journée a été gâchée par les travaux.  De plus, les hôtels avoisinants sont aussi en travaux. La musique de la piscine, qui est pourtant très forte, ne couvre pas les bruits des marteaux piqueurs, scies  sauteuses … c’est vraiment dommage. Cela gâche tout.

Cher @daufincaf,    Merci d’avoir partagé votre avis. Nous sommes vraiment désolés d’apprendre que votre séjour a été perturbé par le bruit des travaux, à la fois dans l’hôtel et aux alentours.    Veuillez accepter nos sincères excuses pour la gêne occasionnée par les travaux dans la zone de la piscine. Ces travaux ont été réalisés dans un souci de sécurité pour nos clients, même si nous comprenons parfaitement à quel point cela a pu affecter votre expérience. En ce qui concerne les travaux des établissements voisins, nous n’avions malheureusement pas été informés de leur calendrier à l’avance, et nous comprenons combien cela a pu être frustrant.    Votre confort est très important pour nous, et nous regrettons que votre séjour n’ait pas été aussi paisible qu’il aurait dû l’être. Nous espérons avoir l’opportunité de vous accueillir de nouveau dans le futur, lorsque l’environnement sera plus calme et que vous pourrez profiter pleinement de l’expérience que nous souhaitons offrir. Parfait pour profiter du soleil, de la mer, et des activités locales.Nous avons été surclassé compte tenu du nombre de nuits ( 14 nuits) ce qui a été exceptionnel.Les chambres sont exceptionnellement confortables avec un style moderne.Le personnel est trés attentionné et professionnel, ce qui rend le séjour encore plus agréable. We stayed at the Shelborne By Proper and it is a gem in the Art Deco scene. Not only is it a beautifully restored hotel, the location is ideal. It is on the beach with the Miami Beach Boardwalk adjacent to the pool which put us in easy walking distance of Ocean Drive. Both Collins Ave and Ocean drive offer endless dining and entertainment.     We stayed in an Oceanview King Suite and had a week of restorative relaxation. The on site restaurants were soooo good! You must dine at Pauline. Truly an exquisite experience. I was so impressed with the quality of healthy ingredients wherever we dined on site. Staff in the restaurants and throughout were friendly, down to earth, very service oriented and helpful.     We used the workout facility and the available equipment was impressive. Yoga and sound baths were available but we missed our opportunities to participate.     We spent as much time as we could by the pool, which was very well appointed and again, staff was so accommodating. I wish I could recall names of all the staff who were so genuinely caring. Bellman Adrian was a gem.     We did have some hiccups along the way but when a team is responsive and follows through to make things right, it is easy to move beyond and focus on the wonderful amenities at hand. We will definitely go back for warm weather getaways and I highly recommend it.

My wife and I enjoyed an outstanding experience at the Shelborne Miami Beach this afternoon, due in large part to the exceptional service provided by Anthony Flores!!! 🙇🏽‍♂️    From the moment we sat down, Anthony’s professionalism was evident✨. He was attentive, courteous, and possessed an impressive knowledge of the entire menu. He didn't just recite options he knew every detail by heart and was able to articulate nuanced descriptions of both dishes and cocktails! While waiting for our drinks me and my wife were talking about the beauty of the lobby bar and how the atmosphere felt so whimsical! And he came back to personally check up on us!  What truly set Anthony apart was his personalized guidance. Based on our preferences, he expertly recommended the best selections for both drinks and food. His suggestions were spot-on and elevated our entire meal specially the lobster and plantain roll👌🏾. It was clear he takes genuine pride in his work and in ensuring guests have a memorable visit.    Our excellent time at the Shelborne is directly thanks to Anthony’s outstanding care and expertise. He is a tremendous asset to the hotel’s team💯✨🔥and we will absolutely look for him on our next visit. We highly recommend requesting his section for a top-tier dining experience.    Thank you, Anthony!

We just stayed at the Shelborne from December 19-26, and can highly recommend the property. We stayed on the 10th floor, ocean view suite and had daily housekeeping service AND turn down each night.  The breakfast and pool food delivered ! It was peaceful & Zen like at the pool and beach. Their red snapper ceviche and guac appetizers were beyond delicious 😋! We didn't try their flagship restaurant b.c we were already booked out each night for dinner. We will return and I really don't get the less than stellar reviews.

VERY DISAPPOINTING   Just finishing up a 3 night stay and we won’t return.  This is a typical chain hotel exuding style over substance. It’s like IKEA heaven throughout with modern design furnishings that look great in a magazine or on a glitzy insta post but lack comfort and luxury to be enjoyed at leisure.   We paid premium for an oceanfront suite with balcony. Don’t bother as the ocean is way beyond the front of the room with pool, an ugly accommodation block a garden and a public walkway between you and the beach!   Additionally the room was billed as a corner suite with the idea of views and two balconies. Both faced the service entrance with constant noise from staff and maintenance who shouted and laughed at all hours of day and night.  There is construction all around the hotel too.  The room again was big on style low on comfort. The TV remotes worked intermittently due to placing of receiver in a hidden position. The dimmer light switches were tricky to operate. There was a coffee machine with no cups. The tiny fridge only had beverage for sale no room to store water. The shower was good but the shower curtain one of those cheap plastic types like you get at Walmart!  There’s no dressing table or any mirror to sit at to do hair or makeup. There’s one tiny and I mean tiny trash can in the bathroom.   One of the other main gripes was the intermittent failure of the elevators. When one worked the other didn’t so often long queues and wait times all through our stay.  There seems to be no service lift so the small lifts were often shared with laundry/cleaning/room service.    Apart from the comfortable bed and great shower there’s nothing that makes this a luxurious suite and was definitely not worth the premium price we paid.    The staff were pleasant though and the beach team in particular were great (especially Andrew) and when we complained about Tv remotes or lack of coffee cups the guest services team and their colleagues were very polite and prompt to deal with.    This hotel isn’t 5 star and is over styled at the expense of basics and comfort.
Nice renovation, food was expensive but nice. Staff were helpful on the most part. Loved the beach chairs and crew looking after guests there. I’d stay again.
We had a wonderful stay! The hotel is absolutely gorgeous! The staff were extremely sweet with our two girls and the food was delicious!     The beach structure and pool were a delight!

Good: Bel hôtel de Miami Beach avec tout ce que l’on peut souhaiter piscine et plage privée mais ça ne fait pas tout    Bad: Pas de douche possible dans l’hôtel une fois la chambre rendue alors que l’hôtel est au bord de mer, même à la piscine il n’y a pas de douche, même pas un malheureux jet d’eau pour se rincer. On nous a proposé d’aller nous laver à la douche municipale du front de mer !!!! Après avoir payé 800 euros par jour on nous propose d’aller nous laver à la douche de la ville sans scrupule. Nous pouvions prendre un late check out qui n’allait que jusque 17h alors que notre avion était à 23h. Même en payant 175 euros de plus il nous fallait nous changer dans les toilettes du restaurant avant de partir ! Jamais vu ça ! Rien à voir avec une prestation 5 étoiles. 30 minutes avant l’heure du Check out la carte n’ouvrait plus la chambre. Nous n’avions plus de carte pour accéder de l’hôtel à la plage une fois le Check out fait. Les bagages restent dans le hall Check out = tu ne fais plus partie de l’hôtel et tu pars C’est incompréhensible et nous avions jamais vu ça. Chambre petit avec peu de rangement Salle de bain très petite sans fenêtre et pas d’espace pour poser les affaires

Love, love, love the new Shelborne by Proper! They did a fantastic job with this hotel renovation. The pool area is very comfortable, and the front desk staff were friendly and helpful.    I stayed shortly after the hotel opened and am just now getting around to writing this review. Based on my notes from the stay, there were a few things I hope have been corrected by now:    The hallway lighting was extremely bright and came through all the seams of the door, making it feel like a night light was on all night.  In my double-bed room, there was no place to hang used/wet towels.  The lighting inside the room was not very self-explanatory.  Signage directing guests to the pool was poor.  Bell service at the entrance did not open the very heavy front door for me at any time.  On a positive note, a big shout-out to Mark and Gabriel at the bar—they made guests feel very welcome. Josh, the pool server, was very attentive, and the beach staff were also warm and welcoming.    Overall, a beautiful property with great potential, and I’m excited to see how it continues to improve.

Good: Ein wunderbares Hotel. Ausstattung, Personal, Pool, Strandservice, Leihfahrräder - vom Feinsten! Wir würden jederzeit wieder buchen!!!!    Bad: Die Sauberkeit im Zimmer ist gut, aber zu ausgezeichnet fehlt es noch ein wenig....

So we booked from 27th to 31 DEC 2025, and knew ahead of time the cost was going to be high during this time.  We booked the cheapest room, city view 2 queens.  Arrival and check in was quick, the person checking us in was quick and efficient but that's about it.  There are one set of elevators for the main tower.  We were on fifth floor facing the roof of the hotel out the side.  As someone said in a review, the room, while large, lacked any real furniture but the two beds which were very comfortable as the mattress is newish.  (hotel really just opened up last winter 2025).  Opposite the beds are simple metal storage unit like something at Ikea.  There is a tiny table with two basic chairs.  No furniture for the large space to lounge or sit.  Other than a few shelves on the metal bookcase, there are no hooks and especially bad no hooks in the small bathroom at all.  You have to put hand towels or towels over shower door.  The vanity is super small with no counter space.  Very odd.  There are built ins for hanging clothes though.  We also experience very spotty housekeeping.  One day they didn't come and had to call, she did quick work and left incomplete set of towels and didn't empty trash.  Next day they came at 6pm when we were getting ready for dinner.  Odd. I did mention this to desk.  The bar area was ok, and semi busy at night for drinks.  In morning it was just used for coffee and tea.  We ate breakfast once in cafe by pool and luckily got in before huge lines formed.  The breakfast was unremarkable and expensive.  We ended up going to the Pura Vida down the street to order great breakfast on the app and bring it back to eat in the bar area.  Best tip right there.  The pool area was the best part -- great staff.  You should get there 8AM to get some chairs and they will set it up -- please tip them.  Great service although you have to really call their attention to come take an order.  Beach was nice although they will set up chairs and umbrella in this super crowded area with others and facing away from beach.  Usually we prefer the beach but from where you sat you couldn't see it and faced the sun.  The pool was the spot to be.  We never had anyone really greet or care to ask how stay was going.  The staff at times seemed to still be trying to coordinate their service with each other.  Our last day we smelled raw sewage coming from the side of the property on sidewalk and had to sidestep it -- what was that about.  In final, it has nice pool spot with calm music and not a crazy scene like some larger hotels have with loud music so that alone is work but the value is not here for this place, sadly.
Good: The staff was exceptional. The food quality and service were unmatched. We had a fantastic time.    Bad: Nothing. We loved everything about this place. Can't beat it.

The Shelbourne is a nice addition to the South Beach hotel scene.  I definitely felt like I earned some "brand loyalty" from our frequent stays at the Santa Monica location, as Cameron put me in touch with Iara and Adina who were super helpful in helping me find a room that was much better fit for our family than the one we were originally given (which was much appreciated particularly given how busy the week was).  The hotel has some very nice features including a nice beach that is less hectic than some of the others we have stayed at in SoBe and the team there including Tanner, Andrew, and Fernando went out of their way to ensure we were well taken care of.  The breakfasts were amazing and special thanks to Marta and Chris (who also helped me turn down the music at the pool which started a bit early and could be heard in the room), although the hotel can use some more menu options for lunch and daytime snacking.  Marc the bellman and Crispy the concierge were super friendly and helpful.  The guests for the most part were nice and we met a few new friends including several who seemed to have stayed at other Proper locations. The hotel definitely has a few kinks to work out but the staff seemed open to suggestions for improvement.

Wonderful hotel. Charming and attentive staff catering to the needs of the newer Miami crowd.  Original modern approach to design, perfectly well suited to post-Beaux Arts Miami. Rooms and common areas beautifully appointed with substancial furnishings. The color palette is very rich and understated. Gorgeous uniforms. A lot of care went into this revival. Appreciate the Aesop bath products, and all the small details that make this place sing. Great pool experience, didn’t even make it to the beach. Excellent poolside menu - lovely fresh and delicious salads. Try the grilled Mahi Mahi basket with farm greens. Love the stackable containers the food is served in. Special thanks to Chris, the host and manager who served us the most perfect breakfast at Pauline’s, the main restaurant.
Amazing hotel. Beautiful design, super nice staffs. Breakfast & pool side food was amazing too, but hope they had more menu.

I was out of my room from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m., yet the room was not cleaned. At 5:00 p.m., before leaving for dinner, I went downstairs to request housekeeping. When I returned at 9:00 p.m., the room was cleaned half way—sand on the floor and dirty dishes still present.    I went back downstairs simply to request water, which arrived around 9:30 p.m. Then passed 11:00 p.m., a housekeeper knocked on my door asking to clean the room, waking up my child in the process. I understand that hotels can get busy, but offering to clean a room at 11:00 at night is completely unacceptable. If management truly wanted to make things right, someone should have been sent earlier in the evening. Instead, the situation was handled poorly and made worse.    We also had a bad experience at the restaurant. We came for breakfast, and despite the restaurant being about 75% empty, we waited 20 minutes to be seated. I eventually spoke to a gentleman named Michael, who said he was a manager. He told us they were working to get everyone seated and then walked away. We left shortly after, as there are many nearby options with far better service.    Another thing my wife noticed was the overall lack of hospitality. Staff never greeted guests—no “good morning,” “good afternoon,” or even a smile when passing by. The only exception was the bell staff and valet team, who were genuinely friendly and helpful. I feel bad leaving a one-star review because of them.   I did tip absolutely everyone that did something positive including leaving money in the room before every cleaning.       That said, this is not, by any standard, a high-end hotel experience Ritz a block away was cheaper for the same nights.  I used my Marriott points to stay at this hotel  since they accept them.   Don’t expect any Marriott perks like free breakfast or bonus points.  They don’t honor them at this place (Titanium status).   I travel frequently and stay over 80 nights a year in hotels on average. Management here clearly needs serious training. While I love the location, there are too many better options in the same proximity , and it will be an easy decision not to stay here again.
